nodes.context: Palestinian Canadian family of gangsters involved in drug trafficking and violence, based in Surrey, British Columbia.
Three brothers (Sandip, Balraj, Paul) involved in drug trafficking and violence, formerly part of the Punjabi mafia.
Organized crime group of Punjabi origin active in British Columbia, associated with Bindy Johal.
Street gang based in British Columbia; Mahmoud Alkhalil was a member.
Outlaw motorcycle club involved in a shoot-out with Mahmoud Alkhalil at Loft Six nightclub in 2003.
Drug trafficking group associated with the Duhre brothers; Martin Naud worked for them.
Mexican drug cartel; Nabil Alkhalil served as liaison for the Alkhalil family.
A criminal group led by Rabih Alkhalil and Larry Amero, involved in drug trafficking and murder.
Montreal-based gang that controls the Port of Montreal; member Shane Maloney was key in the Wolfpack.

edges.context: Long-standing feud over drug territory and personal vendetta starting with Khalil Alkhalil's murder.
The Duhre brothers were once part of the Punjabi mafia and worked alongside Bindy Johal.
Mahmoud Alkhalil was a member of the Independent Soldiers street gang.
Hells Angels killed Mahmoud Alkhalil in a shoot-out at Loft Six nightclub.
Martin Naud worked as a drug dealer for the Dhak-Duhre group, which is associated with the Duhre brothers.
Nabil Alkhalil served as the Alkhalil family's liaison with the Sinaloa Cartel.
Wolfpack distributed cocaine from the Sinaloa Cartel.
Shane Maloney of the West End Gang was a key member of the Wolfpack.
Larry Amero, a Hells Angel, co-led the Wolfpack with Alkhalil.
Wolfpack members murdered Sandip Duhre and Suhhveer Dhak of the Dhak-Duhre group.
